reason: DROP TABLE is the SQL DDL statement that removes a relation; DELETE removes rows, ALTER modifies structure, ERASE is not SQL.
Referential integrity: every non-null foreign-key value must match an existing primary-key value in the referenced table.
ALTER INDEX is the real SQL command (e.g. Oracle ALTER INDEX ... STORAGE/REBUILD); the other three are not SQL commands.
Data integrity is defined as the correctness/accuracy and completeness of data; security is about access control, independence about schema decoupling.
DML covers SELECT/INSERT/DELETE (and UPDATE), so retrieval, insertion and deletion are all enabled -> All of the above.
Compiled and ran with g++ -std=c++17: prints 'Hii World' (NULL is 0, so the if is false).
Compiled and ran: prints 'PQR'. A && (B>C) is false; the else-if is an assignment A = (B || C==20) = 1, which is truthy.
Compiled and ran: outputs 4, 6, 8, 10 on separate lines; i=j++ makes j odd in the body so j==8 never holds and j advances by 2 per iteration.
Compiled and ran: prints '1 2 3 4 5 ' (space-separated).
Compiled and ran: prints 10. The ';' after the for-header empties the loop body; the brace block runs once with i already 10.

Question-bank convention counts 4 loop types (while, do-while, for, nested); pedantically C++ has 3 statement forms plus range-based for, so the item is loosely worded but 4 is the only defensible listed answer.
break exits the enclosing loop immediately, cancelling the remaining iterations; 'skips a particular iteration' is continue, and the program does not terminate.
Compiled and ran with a counter: printf is unreachable dead code after if/else (continue or break always taken), so 'Hello' prints 0 times.
Compiled and ran with a counter: 125 iterations (j = 1,3,...,249; exits at j = 251).
& is bitwise AND; the logical operators in C/C++ are &&, || and !.
BODMAS ordering of the four listed operators is Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction.
Compiled and ran: prints 6. Trailing ';' empties the loop body, so printf runs once after the loop with i == 6.
First octet 11 is in 1-126, leading bit 0 -> Class A, default mask 255.0.0.0.

4.0.0.0 is Class A (/8); mask /24 borrows 16 bits -> 2^16 = 65536 subnets; 8 host bits left -> 254 usable hosts. (Under the older 'subtract 2 subnets' convention it would be 65534, but the modern/standard count is 2^borrowed.)
130.0.0.0 is Class B (/16); 255.255.128.0 is /17, so 1 borrowed bit -> 2 subnets; 15 host bits -> 32766 usable hosts.
